channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sydney Swans pay tribute to Bondi victims as Charlie Curnow returns against Carlton
item.description: The 2026 AFL season kicked off at the SCG with a poignant tribute to the Bondi Beach shooting victims, as Sydney Swans CEO Matthew Pavlich led a minute of silence in honour of the 15 lives lost and re...

item.title: Investigation into death of 9-year-old boy following surgery by suspended surgeon
item.description: A nine-year-old boy, Jack Moate, died in 2015 after undergoing surgery performed by Kuldeep Stohr, a surgeon who had previously been suspended. Jack suffered significant blood loss during the procedur...

item.title: House committee supports private sector initiative to launch Nigeria’s first Green and Climate Finance Bank
item.description: The House of Representatives Committee on Renewable Energy don show support for the plan to set up Nigeria's first Green and Climate Finance Bank. The committee chairman, Afam Ogene (LP, Anambra), tal...

item.title: Ukraine’s F-16 fleet faced temporary missile shortages amid winter attacks
item.description: Ukraine’s F-16 fighter jets experienced a critical shortage of US-made AIM-9 Sidewinder missiles for over three weeks from late November to mid-December 2025, leaving the air force vulnerable as Russi...

item.title: Kenya Leads Trilateral Mediation Workshop with UK and Finland to Strengthen Peace Efforts
item.description: Kenya recently hosted senior diplomats and mediation experts from the United Kingdom and Finland in Nairobi for a trilateral workshop focused on strengthening global conflict resolution through mediat...

item.title: Iwobi Scores Stunning Goal as Fulham Strengthens European Ambition
item.description: Alex Iwobi don shine well well for Fulham side as dem beat Tottenham Hotspur for Premier League. Di former Arsenal player talk say di goal wey e score na ‘unique’, as e side-foot ball from outside di ...

item.title: Gold mining success in West Africa amid record prices
item.description: Gold production in West Africa is experiencing a major upswing, with companies such as Barrick, B2Gold, and Resolute Mining emerging as major beneficiaries. Despite the instability of gold prices in 2...

item.title: US Shifts Policy to Engage Sahel Juntas Amid Rising Jihadist Threats
item.description: This piece from the Council on Foreign Relations analyse how the United States don dey change im approach to the three junta-led countries for the Sahel - Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger. Since these mi...

item.title: Security lockdown in Karachi continues after deadly US consulate protest
item.description: Several key roads in Karachi have been closed for the fourth consecutive day due to heavy security arrangements following violent protests outside the US consulate. The unrest erupted after the assass...

item.title: OpenAI to implement stronger safety measures after Tumbler Ridge shootings
item.description: Following the tragic mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, B.C., Federal Artificial Intelligence Minister Evan Solomon announced that OpenAI has agreed to strengthen its safety protocols. The CEO of OpenAI,...

item.title: Economists Warn of Economic Risks Amid U.S.-Israel Conflict with Iran
item.description: Economists are expressing serious concerns about the economic consequences of the ongoing U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, emphasizing that the impact could extend beyond immediate oil price fluctuati...

item.title: Rijksmuseum Authenticates 1633 Rembrandt Painting 'Vision of Zacharias in the Temple' After 65 Years in Private Collection
item.description: Researchers at the Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam have officially confirmed that the painting 'Vision of Zacharias in the Temple,' dated 1633, is an authentic early work by Rembrandt van Rijn. The artwork, ...

item.title: Proteas Reflect on Heavy T20 World Cup Semi-Final Loss to New Zealand
item.description: South Africa’s T20 World Cup campaign, which had been flawless up to the semi-finals, ended abruptly with a nine-wicket defeat against New Zealand at Eden Gardens. Proteas captain Aiden Markram descri...

item.title: Mark Carney Outlines Key Areas of Australia-Canada Collaboration as Middle Powers
item.description: Mark Carney has outlined several key areas of cooperation between Australia and Canada as part of their role as middle powers in a shifting global order. Speaking to the federal parliament with both h...

item.title: MultiChoice Confirms Showmax Streaming Service Will Be Discontinued
item.description: MultiChoice has confirmed that it will be discontinuing Showmax, its popular streaming service in Africa. According to the company, this decision comes after a thorough internal review and strategic a...

item.title: Tanzania Pushes for Transparency and Efficiency in Oil and Gas Sector
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The Minister of Energy, Deogratius Ndejembi, lately held a meeting with all key institutions under the Ministry of Energy responsible for handling Tanzania's oil and gas sector. The di...

item.title: Oscar Allen and Zane Zakostelsky set to debut for Brisbane Lions in opening AFL round
item.description: Former West Coast captain Oscar Allen is set to make his debut for the reigning premiers, the Brisbane Lions, this Saturday in their opening AFL fixture against the Western Bulldogs. Allen, who has be...

item.title: Two Students Injured in School Bus Collision with Tractor-Trailer in London
item.description: Two students were injured when their school bus was T-boned by a tractor-trailer in London, Ontario. The incident occurred in the afternoon, and emergency services responded quickly to assist those in...

item.title: Northern Ontario man charged with impaired driving and drug offences after Highway 17 stop
item.description: Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) officers in Northern Ontario have charged a driver with impaired driving and drug-related offences following a traffic stop on Highway 17. The stop occurred when office...

item.title: Security Guard Dies During Las Piñas Homeowners’ Association Dispute
item.description: A violent confrontation erupted at BF Resort Village in Las Piñas on Wednesday during a leadership dispute between two homeowners’ associations. A court sheriff, backed by police officers, attempted t...
